example: Support from family and friends acts as a buffer against stress.
collocations: act/serve as a buffer|buffer against sth|buffer zone|provide a buffer
idioms: hit the buffers :: if a plan, somebody’s career, etc. hits the buffers, it suddenly stops being successful :: The former tennis star’s comeback trail hit the buffers yesterday when she lost in straight sets.

definition: small insect (bọ)|[informal]mild illness (bệnh nhẹ dễ lây)|computer fault (lỗi phần mềm)
example: There's a bug crawling up your arm.|a flu bug|The software is full of bugs.
collocations: bug crawls/bites|flu/stomach bug|software/computer bug|fix/find a bug
idioms: bug the hell/crap/shit out of somebody :: to annoy somebody very much :: The song just bugs the hell out of me.

definition: main part (phần lớn)|large size/amount (khối lượng lớn)
example: The bulk of the population lives in cities.|Despite its bulk and weight, the car is extremely fast.
collocations: the bulk of sth|great/large bulk|in bulk|bulk order/buying
idioms: bulk large :: to be the most important part of something :: The issue is likely to bulk large at the next summit meeting.

definition: small metal object fired from a gun (viên đạn)
example: He was found to have a single bullet wound in his chest.
idioms: bite the bullet :: to start to deal with an unpleasant or difficult situation which cannot be avoided :: I wasn’t happy with the way my career was going so I decided to bite the bullet and look for another job.$$dodge a/the bullet | dodge bullets :: to only just avoid getting hurt in a dangerous situation :: South Texas dodged a bullet with no direct hit from Hurricane Emily.

definition: heavy duty or worry (gánh nặng)
example: to bear/carry/ease/reduce/share the burden
collocations: bear/carry/share the burden|ease/reduce the burden|financial/heavy burden|burden on sb

definition: complex official system (bộ máy quan liêu/thủ tục rườm rà)
example: unnecessary/excessive bureaucracy

definition: burying a dead body (sự chôn cất)
example: a burial place/mound/site

definition: extreme tiredness from overwork (kiệt sức vì làm quá sức)
example: job burnout

definition: break open suddenly (vỡ/bục ra)|move suddenly (lao vào)|be very full of sth (đầy ắp)
example: That balloon will burst if you blow it up any more.|He burst into the room without knocking.|The roads are bursting with cars.
collocations: burst open|burst into/out of sth|burst with pride/anger/cars|burst into tears/laughter
idioms: be bursting/bulging at the seams :: to be very full, especially of people :: Los Angeles is bursting at the seams with would-be actors.|The film is bursting at the seams with good performances.$$be bursting to do something :: to want to do something so much that you can hardly stop yourself :: She was bursting to tell him the good news.

definition: secondary result of a process (sản phẩm phụ/hệ quả phụ)
example: One of the by-products of unemployment is an increase in crime.

definition: small room on a ship (ca-bin tàu)|passenger area in a plane (khoang máy bay)
example: I lay in my cabin feeling miserably seasick.|the first-class cabin

definition: top government group (nội các)|storage furniture (tủ)
example: a cabinet meeting|kitchen cabinets

definition: using numbers (tính toán)|careful judgement (sự cân nhắc)
example: Cathy did a rough calculation.|Decisions are shaped by political calculations.

definition: man-made waterway (kênh đào)
example: the Panama/Suez Canal

definition: wax light stick (nến)
example: a flickering candle
collocations: light/blow out a candle|flickering/burning candle|candle flame/light
idioms: burn the candle at both ends :: to become very tired by trying to do too many things and going to bed late and getting up early$$cannot hold a candle to somebody/something :: is not as good as somebody or something else :: His singing can't hold a candle to yours.

definition: strong cloth (vải bạt)|painting surface/work (tranh/vải vẽ)
example: tents made from heavy canvas|a sale of the artist’s early canvases
idioms: a blank canvas/slate :: a person or thing that has the potential to be developed or changed in many different ways :: The building is a blank canvas for a clever investor to potentially make a lot of money.|Alice was a blank slate in the first film because she had memory loss and knew nothing about herself.$$under canvas :: in a tent :: They spent the night under canvas.

definition: ability to do sth (khả năng)
example: Age affects the range of a person's capabilities.
collocations: have/lack capability|capability to do sth|military/technical capability|range of capabilities

definition: private-profit economy (chủ nghĩa tư bản)
example: the growth of industrial capitalism in the West
collocations: industrial/free-market capitalism|growth/development of capitalism|capitalism in sth

definition: based on capitalism (thuộc tư bản chủ nghĩa)
example: a capitalist society/system/economy

definition: chemical element (cacbon)|CO2 in climate context (khí carbon/CO2)
example: carbon fibre|carbon emissions

definition: relating to the heart and blood vessels (thuộc tim mạch)
example: cardiovascular disease

definition: goods being carried (hàng hóa vận chuyển)
example: The tanker began to spill its cargo of oil.
collocations: carry/haul/move cargo|load/unload cargo|bulk/heavy cargo|cargo ship/aircraft/carrier|cargo of oil/goods

definition: train passenger section (toa tàu)|horse-drawn vehicle (xe ngựa)
example: a railway carriage|a horse-drawn carriage

definition: cut shapes or words (chạm/khắc)|slice cooked meat (thái thịt)
example:
a carved doorway

They carved their initials on the desk.|Who's going to carve the turkey?
collocations: carve wood/stone|carve initials/name into sth|carve the turkey/meat
idioms: carved/set in stone :: unable to be changed :: People should remember that our proposals aren't set in stone.|These rules are not set in stone.

definition: small waterfall (thác nhỏ)
example: a small cascade
collocations: small/rocky cascade|cascade of water|series of cascades

definition: gambling place (sòng bạc)
example: a new hotel and casino in Las Vegas

definition: not formal (không trang trọng)
example: casual clothes (= comfortable clothes that you choose to wear in your free time)

definition: person killed or injured (người thương vong)|victim of an event (nạn nhân)|emergency department (khoa cấp cứu)
example: Our primary objective is reducing road casualties.|She became a casualty (victim) of the reduction in part-time work (= she lost her job).|The victims were rushed to casualty.

definition: complete item list (danh mục)
example: to consult the library catalogue

definition: in a clear and definite way (dứt khoát/rõ ràng)
example: He categorically rejected our offer.

definition: provide food for an event (cung cấp đồ ăn)
example: Most of our work now involves catering for weddings.

definition: farm cows and bulls (gia súc/bò)
example: a herd of cattle

definition: care to avoid risk (sự thận trọng)|[formal]warning about risk (lời cảnh báo)
example: extreme/great caution|a word/note of caution
collocations: great/extreme caution|exercise/use caution|word/note of caution|caution against sth
idioms: throw/cast caution to the wind(s) :: to stop caring about how dangerous something might be; to start taking risks :: He threw caution to the wind and dived into the water after the child.

definition: careful to avoid risk (thận trọng)
example: The government has been cautious in its response to the report.

definition: large natural hole (hang động)
example: the mouth (= the entrance) of the cave

definition: stop happening/existing (chấm dứt)
example: Welfare payments cease as soon as an individual starts a job.
idioms: wonders will never cease :: a phrase used to express surprise and pleasure at something :: ‘I've cleaned my room.’ ‘Wonders will never cease!’

definition: place for burying dead people (nghĩa trang)
example: He was buried in a private cemetery.
collocations: cemetery gates/plot|be buried in a cemetery|private/military cemetery

definition: being sure (sự chắc chắn)
example: There is no certainty that the president's removal would end the civil war.

definition: official proof document (giấy chứng nhận)
example: a birth/marriage/death certificate

definition: difficult but interesting (thử thách)
example: challenging work/questions/problems

definition: formal meeting hall (phòng họp lớn)|part of parliament (viện/nghị viện)
example: The members left the council chamber.|the Lower/Upper Chamber (= in Britain, the House of Commons/House of Lords)

definition: top sports competition or title (giải/chức vô địch)
example: the National Basketball Association Championship

definition: complete disorder (hỗn loạn)
example: economic/political/domestic chaos

definition: small Christian prayer/service place (nhà nguyện)
example: a college chapel

definition: often seen in sb/sth (đặc trưng)|feature that helps identify sb/sth (đặc điểm)
example: Community support of families is characteristic (uncharacteristic) of many societies.|There were few similarities in the brothers' physical characteristics.

definition: be typical of (đặc trưng cho)|describe the qualities of sth (mô tả đặc điểm)
example: the rolling hills that characterize this part of England|How would you characterize the mood of the 1990s?
collocations: characterize a period/place|characterize sth as sth|be characterized by sth

definition: pleasing attractiveness (sức hút/vẻ duyên dáng)
example: He was a man of great charm.
idioms: third time is the charm :: used when you have failed to do something twice and hope that you will succeed the third time$$work like a charm :: to be immediately and completely successful :: Those new pills you gave me worked like a charm.|Her strategy worked like a charm.

definition: very pleasant or attractive (duyên dáng/dễ thương)
example: The cottage is tiny, but it's charming.

definition: rights/principles document (hiến chương)|founding permission (giấy phép thành lập)
example: the United Nations Charter (constitution)|The Royal College received its charter as a university in 1967.
collocations: UN/Social Charter|charter of rights|receive/grant a charter|royal charter

definition: run/drive after sb/sth (đuổi theo)|force sb/sth away (đuổi đi)
example: The thieves were caught by police after a short chase.|Chase the cat out—we don't want her in the kitchen.
collocations: police/car chase|chase sb/sth|chase after sb/sth|chase sb/sth out/away
idioms: chase your (own) tail :: to be very busy but in fact achieve very little$$cut to the chase :: to stop wasting time and start talking about the most important thing :: Right, let's cut to the chase. How much is it going to cost?

definition: side of the face below the eye (má)
example: chubby/rosy/pink cheeks
collocations: rosy/pink/chubby cheeks|kiss sb on the cheek|cheek bone
idioms: cheek by jowl (with somebody/something) :: very close to somebody/something :: The guests, packed cheek by jowl, parted as he entered.$$put roses in somebody’s cheeks :: to make somebody look healthy

definition: shout to show support or joy (tiếng reo hò/cổ vũ)
example:
A great cheer (boo) went up from the crowd.

We all cheered (boo) as the team came onto the field.
collocations: give/raise a cheer|great/loud cheer|cheer for sb/team|cheer sb on

definition: science of substances and their reactions (hóa học)
example: a degree in chemistry

definition: singing group (dàn hợp xướng)
example: She sings in the school choir.
collocations: school/church choir|join/sing in a choir|choir practice
idioms: preach to the choir :: to speak to people in support of views that they already hold :: I realize I may be preaching to the choir, but I think he's gorgeous.

definition: cut into pieces (chặt/thái nhỏ)
example: He was chopping logs for firewood.
idioms: bust your chops/butt/hump (doing something/to do something) :: to make a great effort to do something :: I busted my chops to get into law school.|These guys were busting their butts on the field, trying to excel at athletics.|It’s a problem which nobody is going to bust a gut trying to solve.$$bust a gut (doing something/to do something) :: to make a great effort to do something :: It’s a problem which nobody is going to bust a gut trying to solve.|I busted my chops to get into law school.|These guys were busting their butts on the field, trying to excel at athletics.

definition: regular household task (việc vặt/việc nhà)
example: doing the household/domestic chores

definition: long-lasting and hard to cure/solve (mạn tính/kéo dài)
example: chronic (acute) bronchitis/arthritis/asthma

definition: thick piece (miếng lớn)|large amount (phần lớn)
example: a chunk of cheese/masonry|a substantial chunk of our profits
collocations: chunk of cheese/rock/meat|large/substantial chunk of sth
idioms: blow chunks :: to vomit

definition: route around a place (vòng đua/tuyến vòng)|path for electric current (mạch điện)
example: The race ended with eight laps of a city centre circuit.|an electrical circuit
collocations: race/city circuit|complete a circuit|electrical/electric circuit|circuit board

definition: move around continuously (lưu thông)|spread/pass around (lan truyền/chuyền tay)
example: The condition prevents the blood from circulating freely.|Rumours began to circulate about his financial problems.
collocations: blood/air circulates|circulate freely|rumours/information circulate|circulate sth among/to sb

definition: movement around a system (sự lưu thông)|spread or passing around (sự lưu hành/lan truyền)|number of copies sold (số lượng phát hành)
example: Regular exercise will improve blood circulation.|the circulation of money/information/ideas|a daily circulation of more than one million
collocations: blood/air circulation|circulation of money/information|daily/weekly circulation|improve circulation

definition: relating to blood flow around the body (thuộc tuần hoàn máu)
example: the circulatory system

definition: legal status as a citizen (quyền công dân/quốc tịch)|duties of being a citizen (tư cách công dân)
example: They were granted full French citizenship.|an education that prepares young people for citizenship

definition: related to a town/city or citizens (thuộc đô thị/công dân)
example: civic buildings/leaders

definition: not military/police (dân sự)|person not in military/police (thường dân)
example: He left the army and returned to civilian life.|Two soldiers and one civilian were killed in the explosion.

definition: developed society or culture (nền văn minh)|modern human society (xã hội văn minh)
example: the civilizations of ancient Greece and Rome|Environmental damage threatens the whole of civilization.

definition: [formal]make clear (làm rõ)
example: to clarify a situation/problem/issue
collocations: clarify situation/problem/issue|clarify position/matters|ask sb to clarify sth

definition: being clear (sự rõ ràng)
example: a lack of clarity in the law
collocations: lack of clarity|greater/absolute clarity|clarity of thought/expression|provide/bring clarity

definition: fight, disagreement, or opposition (xung đột/trái ngược)|time conflict (trùng lịch)|metal noise (tiếng va chạm kim loại)
example:
Clashes broke out between police and demonstrators.

a clash (conflict) of interests/opinions/cultures|a clash in the timetable/schedule|a clash of cymbals/swords
collocations: clash between/with sb|clash over sth|clash of interests/opinions/cultures|timetable/schedule clash|clash of cymbals/swords

definition: putting into groups (sự phân loại)
example: a style of music that defies classification (= is like no other)

definition: put into groups (phân loại)
example: The books in the library are classified according to subject.
collocations: classify sth by/according to sth|classify sth as sth|be classified into groups

definition: shop/hotel/office worker (nhân viên cửa hàng, khách sạn hoặc văn phòng)
example: The clerk at the counter gave me too little change.
collocations: office/hotel/sales clerk|clerk at the counter|court clerk

definition: steep rock side (vách đá)
example: the cliff edge/top
collocations: cliff edge/top/face|steep/high cliff|fall off a cliff
idioms: fall off a cliff :: to decrease quickly and suddenly in quantity or quality :: Sales fell off a cliff in the first quarter of the year.

definition: hold tightly (bám chặt)|stick closely (dính sát)|stay close emotionally (bám víu)
example: survivors clinging to a raft|a dress that clings (= fits closely and shows the shape of your body)|After her mother's death, Sara clung to her aunt more than ever.
collocations: cling to sth/sb|cling tightly/desperately|clinging dress|cling to hope/belief

definition: place/time for special medical care (phòng khám/buổi khám)
example: He was treated at the hospital's eye clinic.

definition: related to treating patients (lâm sàng)
example: clinical research (= done on patients, not just considering theory)

definition: short film/video part (đoạn phim ngắn)
example: Here is a clip from her latest movie.
collocations: video/film clip|short clip|clip from sth|watch/show a clip
idioms: clip somebody’s wings :: to limit a person’s freedom or power :: Having a new baby to take care of has clipped her wings.$$at a fast, good, steady, etc. clip :: quickly :: Land prices will rise at a healthy clip.

definition: permanent or temporary closing (sự đóng cửa)
example: factory closures

definition: group close together (cụm/nhóm)
example: The telescope is focused on a dense cluster of stars at the edge of the galaxy.

definition: groups/parties working together (liên minh)
example: The two parties have formed a coalition.

definition: of or near the coast (ven biển)
example: coastal waters/resorts/scenery

definition: mixed drink (cocktail)|small cold food dish (món cocktail)|mixture of things (hỗn hợp)
example: a cocktail bar/cabinet/shaker|a prawn/shrimp cocktail|a lethal cocktail of drugs

definition: related to thinking (nhận thức)
example: a child’s cognitive development

definition: clear, logical and well organized (mạch lạc)|able to talk clearly (tỉnh táo/nói năng mạch lạc)
example: a coherent narrative/account/explanation|She only became coherent (incoherent) again two hours after the attack.

definition: happen at the same time (xảy ra cùng lúc)
example: It's a pity our trips to New York don't coincide.
collocations: coincide with sth|timed/planned to coincide with sth|exactly/roughly coincide

definition: surprising chance happening (sự trùng hợp)
example: a strange/an extraordinary/a remarkable coincidence

definition: work together (hợp tác)|[disapproving]help the enemy (cộng tác với kẻ thù)
example: Researchers around the world are collaborating to develop a new vaccine.|He was accused of collaborating with the enemy.
collocations: collaborate with sb|collaborate on sth|collaborate to do sth|collaborate with the enemy

definition: working together (sự hợp tác)|work made together (sản phẩm hợp tác)|[disapproving]helping the enemy (cộng tác với kẻ thù)
example: It was a collaboration that produced extremely useful results.|The exhibition is a collaboration with the Australian Centre for the Moving Image.|his wartime collaboration with the Nazis
collocations: collaboration with/between sb|close/fruitful collaboration|research/international collaboration|collaboration with the enemy/Nazis

definition: done or shared by a group (tập thể/chung)
example: collective leadership/decision-making/responsibility

definition: person who collects things (người sưu tầm/thu gom)
example: a stamp collector

definition: crash between vehicles/people (vụ va chạm)
example: a collision between two trains
collocations: collision between/with sth|head-on collision|avoid/cause a collision
idioms: be on a collision course (with somebody/something) :: to be in a situation that is almost certain to cause an argument :: I was on a collision course with my boss over the sales figures.

definition: related to overseas rule or settlement (thuộc địa/thực dân)
example: a colonial power

definition: settle in and control another land (thực dân hóa/chiếm thuộc địa)|[biology]spread and start living/growing somewhere (xâm chiếm/cư trú lan rộng)
example: The area was colonized by the Vikings.|The slopes are colonized by flowering plants.
collocations: colonize a country/area/territory|be colonized by plants/bacteria/species

definition: area ruled by another country (thuộc địa)
example: former British colonies
collocations: former/British colony|establish/found a colony|colony of sth

definition: bright and varied (sặc sỡ)
example: colourful shop windows

definition: regular newspaper/magazine writer (người viết chuyên mục)
example: a newspaper columnist

definition: fighting, especially in war (chiến đấu)|stop sth harmful (chống lại)
example: He was killed in combat.|measures to combat crime/inflation/unemployment/disease

definition: funny (hài hước)|picture-story magazine (truyện tranh)
example: a comic monologue/story|superhero comics

definition: person in charge, especially military (chỉ huy)
example: military/allied/field/flight commanders
collocations: military/field/flight commander|commander of sth|commander in chief

definition: begin (bắt đầu)
example: The meeting is scheduled to commence at noon.

definition: live spoken description (bình luận trực tiếp)|written discussion/explanation (bài bình luận/chú giải)
example: a sports commentary|a critical commentary on the final speech of the play
collocations: live/radio/sports commentary|provide/give commentary|critical/political commentary|commentary on sth

definition: live event describer (bình luận viên)|media expert on a subject (nhà bình luận)
example: a television/sports commentator|She's a political commentator for the BBC.

definition: trade, especially between countries (thương mại)
example: Leaders of industry and commerce met at the summit in Paris.
collocations: international/world/global commerce|industry and commerce|commerce between countries

definition: official commission member (ủy viên)
example: the Church Commissioners (= the group of people responsible for controlling the financial affairs of the Church of England)
collocations: police/election/European commissioner|commissioner for sth|appoint a commissioner

definition: group chosen to discuss/decide sth (ủy ban/hội đồng)
example: an executive/advisory/organizing committee
collocations: committee member/meeting|set up/appoint/serve on a committee|advisory/executive committee

definition: turn sth into a product (hàng hóa hóa/thương mại hóa)
example: Christmas has been grossly commodified over the years.

definition: [economics]product for sale (hàng hóa)|[formal]useful thing (thứ có giá trị)
example: rice, flour and other basic commodities|Water is a precious commodity that is often taken for granted in the West.

definition: related to communism (thuộc cộng sản)|run by a Marxist party (do Đảng Cộng sản lãnh đạo)
example: communist ideology|a Communist country/government/leader

definition: person or animal who stays with you (bạn đồng hành)
example: travelling companions
collocations: travelling/constant/close companion|companion on a trip/journey|animal companion

definition: similar enough to judge together (có thể so sánh)
example: A comparable house in the south of the city would cost twice as much.

definition: measured by comparison (tương đối)|based on comparison (mang tính so sánh)
example: Then he was living in comparative (relative) comfort (= compared with others or with his own life at a previous time).|a comparative study of the educational systems of two countries

definition: sympathy and wish to help (lòng trắc ẩn)
example: to feel/show compassion

definition: able to work or exist well together (tương thích/hợp nhau)
example: The new system will be compatible with existing equipment.

definition: force sb to do sth (bắt buộc)
example: The law can compel fathers to make regular payments for their children.
collocations: compel sb to do sth|feel compelled to do sth|be compelled by law

definition: very interesting (cuốn hút)|too strong to resist (khó cưỡng)|convincing (thuyết phục)
example: Her latest book makes compelling reading.|a compelling need/desire|There is no compelling reason to believe him.

definition: make up for loss (bù đắp)|pay for damage (bồi thường)
example: Nothing can compensate (make up for) for the loss of a loved one.|Her lawyers say she should be compensated for the suffering she had been caused.
collocations: compensate for loss/damage|compensate sb for sth|be compensated for sth

definition: money for damage (tiền bồi thường)|pay for work (lương/thù lao)|thing that makes bad situation better (điều bù đắp)
example: to claim/award/receive compensation|Send your CV and current compensation to Executive Search Consultant.|I wish I were young again, but getting older has its compensations.
collocations: claim/award/receive compensation|compensation for damage/injury|current/total compensation|have its compensations

definition: ability to do sth well (năng lực)
example: to gain a high level of competence (incompetence) in English
collocations: gain/demonstrate competence|competence in sth|level of competence|professional/technical competence

definition: able or good enough (có năng lực/đạt yêu cầu)
example: He's very competent (incompetent) in his work.
collocations: competent in/at sth|competent to do sth|highly/perfectly competent|competent worker/manager

definition: in which people/teams/businesses compete (cạnh tranh)|very eager to win (hiếu thắng/có tính cạnh tranh)
example: competitive games/sports|You have to be highly competitive (uncompetitive) to do well in sport these days.

definition: collect and put together (biên soạn/tổng hợp)
example: We are trying to compile a list of suitable people for the job.

definition: go well with and improve sth (bổ sung/làm nổi bật)
example: The excellent menu is complemented by a good wine list.
collocations: complement each other|perfectly/nicely complement sth|complement a menu/style/design

definition: finishing sth (sự hoàn thành)
example: the completion of the new hospital building
collocations: completion of sth|on/upon completion|near completion|successful completion

definition: many connected parts or difficulty (độ phức tạp)

definition: computer main screen (màn hình desktop)

definition: with little hope (tuyệt vọng)|extremely or very much (cực kỳ/rất muốn)

collocations: detach sth from sth|detach yourself from sb/sth|detach soldiers/troops/ships to do sth

definition: evil spirit (ác quỷ)

idioms: between the devil and the deep blue sea :: in a difficult situation where there are two equally unpleasant or unacceptable choices$$sell your soul (to the devil) :: to do anything, even something really bad or dishonest, in return for money, success or power

definition: invent a plan or method (nghĩ ra)

idioms: be devoured by something :: to be filled with a strong emotion that seems to control you :: She was devoured by envy and hatred.

definition: identify illness or problem (chẩn đoán)

definition: identifying illness or problem (sự chẩn đoán)

definition: tell sb what to do (ra lệnh)|control how sth happens (chi phối/quyết định)|say words to be written (đọc cho viết)

definition: all-power ruler (nhà độc tài)

definition: be unlike (khác)

collocations: differ from sth|differ in size/shape/opinion|differ widely/greatly
idioms: agree to differ/disagree :: if two people agree to differ/disagree, they accept that they have different opinions about something, but they decide not to discuss it any longer :: We must just agree to differ on this.|In the end we agreed to disagree.|I think you're wrong. Let's just agree to differ.$$I beg to differ :: used to say politely that you do not agree with something that has just been said :: ‘At least she is good at her job.’ ‘Oh, I beg to differ.’

definition: tell or show the difference (phân biệt)|treat differently, often unfairly (đối xử khác biệt)
example: It's difficult to differentiate (distinguish) between the two varieties.|Rules that differentiate (discriminate) between employees on the basis of gender need to be revised.
collocations: differentiate between A and B|differentiate A from B|differentiate on the basis of sth

definition: respect and self-worth (phẩm giá)

collocations: with dignity|human/personal dignity|lose/keep dignity|dignity of sth
idioms: beneath your dignity :: below what you see as your own importance or worth :: He clearly regarded manual work as beneath his dignity.$$stand on your dignity :: to demand to be treated with the respect that you think that you deserve :: Our teacher never stands on her dignity with us, but treats us all as friends and colleagues.|She was never one to stand on her dignity.

definition: difficult choice or problem (tiến thoái lưỡng nan)

idioms: on the horns of a dilemma :: in a situation in which you have to make a choice between things that are equally unpleasant :: The medical profession’s eagerness for scientific advance had impaled it on the horns of a dilemma, forcing an unnatural choice between science and morality.|The dire economic situation had placed the prime minister on the horns of a dilemma.

definition: size or measurement (kích thước)|aspect (khía cạnh)

definition: become or make smaller/weaker (giảm/suy yếu)|make seem less important (hạ thấp/xem nhẹ)

idioms: (the law of) diminishing returns :: used to refer to a point at which you start achieving less than the value of the time or money you put into something :: Our efforts were producing diminishing returns.|In general, the more exercise you take, the greater the health benefit; but beyond a certain level you get diminishing returns.

definition: put briefly in liquid (nhúng)|go down (hạ xuống/giảm)

collocations: dip sth in/into sth|dip below/to sth|prices/sun dip
idioms: dip into your pocket :: to spend some of your own money on something :: She was forced to dip into her own pocket to pay for the repairs.$$dip a toe in/into something | dip a toe in/into the water :: to start doing something very carefully to see if it will be successful or not :: We decided to dip a toe in the computer games market.

a fairground mirror that distorts your shape
